name: Glazed Almond Brie
recipeIngredient: 1 3/4 oz Wheel imported Brie cheese (well chilled)
Red & green seedless grapes (in clusters, for garnish)
recipeInstructions:
1. With a small, flexible knife, carefully cut away the rind from the top of the chilled cheese.
2. Be careful not to remove or split the rind on the sides, as it will form a wall for the melted cheese.
3. Sift 1 tablespoon of confectioners' sugar evenly over the surface.
4. Arrange almonds gently into place.
5. The prepared cheese can be stored covered loosely with foil and refrigerated up to 2 days in advance.
6. When ready to serve, bring the cheese to room temperature.
7. Preheat the broiler and place a rack 6 inches from the heat source.
8. Line a large baking sheet with aluminum foil.
9. Place the cheese on the lined sheet and sift 1 tablespoon of confectioners' sugar over the almonds.
10. Broil the cheese until it is evenly browned, about 1 to 2 minutes. Rotate if necessary for even browning.
11. Watch carefully so the cheese does not burn.
12. Using foil, lift the cheese and transfer it to a serving platter.
13. Gently remove the foil.
14. Garnish with clusters of grapes.
